text.title: Build vs Buy Decision Framework Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the trade-off-build-vs-buy methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Build MUST be chosen only when the capability creates documented competitive differentiation. Capabilities that competitors can replicate by checkbook MUST default to Buy.
rule.rationale: Engineering capacity is finite; spending it on commodity capability sacrifices the differentiating roadmap.

rule.statement: Decision MUST compute 3-year TCO for Build, Buy, and Hybrid. TCO MUST include engineering, maintenance (20-30% of build / yr), infra, integration, training, support, and exit cost.
rule.rationale: 1-year TCO usually favours Buy; 3-year often shifts; without modelling, the dominant cost is invisible.

rule.statement: Buy / Hybrid decisions MUST include an exit plan: data export format + cadence, contract clauses for early termination, alternative provider list. Lock-in without exit plan is a defect.
rule.rationale: Vendor lock-in shows up at renewal time as a price spike; the only defence is a credible exit option.

rule.statement: Buy decisions MUST be preceded by a time-boxed pilot with explicit pass/fail criteria. A 'we tried it and it worked' verbal report MUST NOT substitute.
rule.rationale: Sales-demo capability rarely matches operational fit; pilot with criteria catches the gap before the multi-year contract.

rule.statement: ADR MUST list rejected options with quantified reasons and a review trigger (vendor price increase > X%, capability becomes commodity, build-cost projection 50% off).
rule.rationale: Without rejected + trigger, the next architect cannot reconstruct the reasoning and the decision drifts unchallenged.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (capability, forecast, vendor shortlist, build estimate), apply r1 → r5 and emit the ADR.
rule.rationale: Order encodes risk: differentiation first, TCO second, exit third, pilot fourth, trigger last.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) core IP must be built, (b) commodity must be bought, (c) pre-revenue exploration. Exit with note.
rule.rationale: Build/buy ceremony where the answer is forced wastes review time.
